messages.content:
CVE-2025-0518 is an out-of-bounds read vulnerability in FFmpeg 7.1, categorized under CWE-125. The flaw arises from unchecked return values that can allow reading of sensitive constants within an executable, specifically in the file libavfilter/af_pan.C. This vulnerability has a MEDIUM severity level, indicating a moderate risk to systems. Exploitation could potentially enable unauthorized access to sensitive data or disrupt system functionality. The issue is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1203. Known vulnerable software includes FFmpeg version 7.1, which is susceptible due to improper bounds checking.

To mitigate this vulnerability, it is recommended to apply the patch available at https://github.com/FFmpeg/FFmpeg/commit/b5b6391d64807578ab872dc58fb8aa621dcfc38a. Additionally, regular updates and monitoring for suspicious activities are advisable to ensure system security. Following mitigation frameworks such as M1050, M1051, and M1048 can further strengthen defenses against potential exploits.
